title: AI has not triggered DeFi ‘hackpocalypse,’ Dragonfly partner says
summary: Total value stolen and median hack size are declining compared to 2025, signaling that the AI hacking apocalypse was a false alarm, argued Dragonfly managing partner Haseeb Qureshi.

title: Hobby-level miner bags $200K solo BTC block with budget Bitaxe rig
summary: A solo Bitcoin miner secured a $200,000 block reward with a budget mining rig, pushing payouts to hobby-level miners to $4.7 million for the past year.

title: US Stocks Slip as Bank Earnings Start Before CPI: Markets Wrap
summary: Stocks slipped as traders refrained from taking on risk on a busy day that kicked off with Wall Street earnings. Testimony from Federal Reserve Chair Kevin Warsh and inflation data are due later.

title: Fed Rate-Hike Bets Mount Before Inflation Data, Warsh Testimony
summary: Bond traders ramped up bets for a July interest-rate hike ahead of US inflation data and an appearance by the head of the Federal Reserve that stand to reinforce the need for action.

title: US Government Moves $288M in Seized Crypto to Coinbase Prime
summary: The seized coins landed at the government's custodian, which stops short of a sale but has revived questions about Trump's no-sell pledge.

title: Stop Over-Prompting: OpenAI’s New GPT-5.6 Guidelines Change Everything
summary: Forget the XML blocks and persistence scripts. OpenAI’s new prompting guide says define the destination, set the stopping conditions, and get out of the way.

title: UK HMRC adopts ‘no gain, no loss’ tax treatment for crypto lending, liquidity pools
summary: HMRC adopts "no gain, no loss" treatment for certain crypto loans and liquidity pool transactions, deferring CGT until economic disposal.

title: US government moves over $288 million in seized bitcoin, ether to Coinbase Prime: Arkham
summary: According to Arkham, U.S. government wallets maintain a large crypto portfolio estimated at over $20 billion, including 324,552 BTC.

title: JPMorgan reports record profits on blockbuster trading haul
summary: Goldman Sachs, JPMorgan, Bank of America, Citigroup and Wells Fargo all have results on Tuesday

title: RIP the US state department
summary: America’s diplomats used to have global sway. Now they are besieged by their own president
